title: silene portensis subsp. rigidula
intro: Silene portensis subsp. rigidula, also known as prickly catchfly, is a flowering plant species in the carnation family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic effects. Herbal preparations of Silene portensis subsp. rigidula can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, including coughs and colds.
